verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Ordered startup implemented; Health-gated transitions; Failures surfaced; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.order_evidence: startServicesOrdered() starts services strictly by declared order — proven with two real TCP services the engine itself spawns and health-polls; the production sequence is postgres -> redb -> Glass/Engine front door.
gate_result.health_evidence: Each transition is gated on a real health probe (TCP connect or command); an already-healthy service is NOT restarted (idempotency proven with a poisoned start command that is never invoked). LIVE: the production re-attach STARTED PostgreSQL 17.10 from the intact durable datadir (clean 2026-07-21 19:45 shutdown) with a user-owned socket dir — the canonical macro-state came back up on this host, then subsequent runs found it healthy.
gate_result.failure_evidence: A dead dependency stops the chain: ok:false with the failing service named, and the dependent never starts.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Real spawned TCP services, a real failure path, and the real PostgreSQL instance brought up live.
gate_result.red_first: gates committed red with the re-attach engine and unit absent (4/4 files failing), green at HEAD (lane 12/12)

proof_summary: ARCHBP-096 is complete: ordered, health-gated startup is implemented and proven — postgres then redb then the front door in dependency order, transitions gated on real health probes with no-restart idempotency, failures surfaced by name; the live re-attach actually restarted PostgreSQL 17.10 from the durable datadir on this host.
